claims.evidence_text: the transpiration started to decrease in both segmented transpiration subgroups at similar VPD break points (STR+: 1.063 and STR−: 1.072 kPa)
claims.claim_summary: Segmented-transpiration durum wheat lines began restricting transpiration (transpiration-rate breakpoint) at VPD of about 1.06-1.07 kPa.

claims.evidence_text: These VPD values are clearly lower than the 2 KPa or greater reported previously in wheat, which included lines selected for the Mediterranean conditions of Australia (Schoppach et al., 2017).
claims.claim_summary: Prior wheat studies reported transpiration-restriction VPD breakpoints of about 2 kPa or greater.

claims.evidence_text: Each plot consisted of six rows 7 m long and 0.2 m apart, with a planting density of 250 seeds m −2.
claims.claim_summary: Durum wheat field trials in Spain used a planting/seeding density of 250 seeds per m2.

claims.evidence_text: Each plot consisted of six rows 7 m long and 0.2 m apart, with a planting density of 250 seeds m −2.
claims.claim_summary: Durum wheat field trials used 0.2 m (20 cm) row spacing.
Plant height of 20 modern semi-dwarf durum wheat lines across nine Mediterranean field trials ranged from about 110 to 157 cm.

claims.evidence_text: g s 190 153 ... 402a 382b ... 242 258 455 424 ... 322a 306b 311b (stomatal conductance (g s ) in μmol m −2 s −1).
claims.claim_summary: Flag-leaf stomatal conductance of durum wheat at post-anthesis ranged from about 153 to 455 μmol m-2 s-1 across trials, increasing as growing conditions improved.

claims.evidence_text: LTR lines exhibit higher grain yield, biomass and greenness under good to optimal agronomical conditions, whereas the STR lines perform better under the most water-limiting trials.
claims.claim_summary: Segmented/limited-transpiration (STR) durum wheat lines performed better (higher yield) under the most water-limited conditions, while linear-transpiration (LTR) lines were superior under good to optimal conditions; limited transpiration confers an advantage under severe drought.
